title: Alaska Native Village Municipal Lands Restoration Act of 2025
short_title: Alaska Native Village Municipal Lands Restoration Act of 2025
plain_summary: This law is about land that belongs to Alaska Native village corporations. Right now, these corporations have to give some of their land to the state of Alaska to save for towns that might be created later. This law says they don't have to give away that land anymore. It also lets them get back land they already gave to the state if both the corporation and the village people agree to it.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Alaska Native Village Municipal Lands Restoration Act of two thousand and twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Reversion of Certain Land Conveyed in Trust to the State of Alaska
sections.text: This section changes part of an older law called the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act. The changes affect what happens with land that Alaska Native Village Corporations must set aside. Here are the key changes. First, if a Village Corporation already gave land to the state to hold in trust before this new law was passed, and no city government was ever created on that land, then the Village Corporation and the village residents can vote together to get their land back. The state must then give the land back to the Village Corporation. Second, starting when this new law takes effect, Village Corporations will not have to give any more land to the state for future city governments. Third, when land goes back to a Village Corporation, the corporation must respect any existing legal rights that other people or groups already have to use that land. This includes any leases, easements for roads, or other agreements that were made while the state held the land in trust. The Village Corporation must also take over any responsibilities that came with those agreements.
